summary: All three attempts failed to resolve any of the four persistent SAST findings (bandit:B301, B403, B404, B603). The code retains the exact same insecure pattern: it imports pickle and subprocess, deserializes attacker-controlled data with pickle.loads without validation, and executes arbitrary commands via subprocess.run using the unpickled values. The fixer loop was ineffective, producing identical code each time with no security improvements.
suggested_actions: Replace pickle with a safe serialization format (e.g., JSON) or implement a cryptographic signature/allowlist to validate deserialized data
Add strict validation of command and args against an allowlist before execution
Implement proper authorization checks beyond basic authentication to address CWE-862
Use subprocess with a fixed command and only allow safe arguments from a predefined list

evidence.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
